deno_json_has_check_task.reason: deno.json contains "check": "deno run -A scripts/check.ts" which runs comprehensive verification (fmt, lint, test, typecheck) via scripts/check.ts.

deno_json_has_test_task.reason: deno.json contains "test": "deno test -A" which runs all tests.

deno_json_has_dev_task.reason: deno.json contains "dev": "deno run --watch -A src/main.ts" which uses --watch flag for watch mode.

deno_json_has_prod_task.reason: deno.json contains "prod": "deno run -A src/main.ts" for production mode.

check_script_created.reason: scripts/check.ts was created containing fmt --check, lint, test, and typecheck sub-commands via Deno.Command.

check_script_exits_on_failure.reason: The script calls Deno.exit(1) when failed.length > 0: "console.error(...); Deno.exit(1);".

existing_config_preserved.reason: The original deno.json had compilerOptions.strict and imports (@std/assert). Both are preserved verbatim in the new deno.json alongside the added tasks section.

tasks_reference_scripts.reason: The most complex task (check) references scripts/check.ts. The other tasks (test, dev, prod) use simple, non-complex inline commands (single deno invocations), which is acceptable per the checklist's…

check_runs_parallel.reason: check.ts uses "await Promise.all(checks.map((c) => runCheck(c.name, c.cmd)))" to run all checks in parallel.

check_buffers_output.reason: Deno.Command is created with stdout: "piped" and stderr: "piped", buffering all output per-command to prevent interleaving.

check_failed_last.reason: The script first loops over [...passed, ...skipped] printing their output, then separately loops over failed checks printing them last with "=== FAILED: <name> ===".

check_no_external_deps.reason: check.ts only uses built-in Deno APIs (Deno.Command, TextDecoder, Deno.exit). No external npm packages or JSR imports.

check_sequential_prerequisites.reason: This project has no build/codegen steps (only src/main.ts with a single console.log). The prerequisite condition does not apply, so no violation.

check_realtime_progress.reason: runCheck prints "[start] ${name}" before launching the command and "[${status}] ${name}" (where status is pass/skip/FAIL) immediately after it completes, giving real-time per-command status.

check_no_output_loss.reason: Both passed/skipped and failed checks print their stdout and stderr. The loops over [...passed, ...skipped] and failed both emit stdout+stderr unconditionally (guarded only by truthiness). No output …
